# reactable.shiny

The goal of reactableShiny is to combine the unique features of the
reactable package with shiny inputs so that users can more easily write
data to databases.

## Installation

You can install the development version of reactableShiny from
[GitHub](https://github.com/) with:

``` r
# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("matthewcurrier/reactableShiny")
```

# Package index

## All functions

- [`annotator_enriched_server()`](https://matthewcurrier.github.io/reactableShiny/reference/annotator_enriched_server.md)
  : Enriched annotator — server
- [`annotator_enriched_ui()`](https://matthewcurrier.github.io/reactableShiny/reference/annotator_enriched_ui.md)
  : Enriched annotator — UI
- [`annotator_super_simple_server()`](https://matthewcurrier.github.io/reactableShiny/reference/annotator_super_simple_server.md)
  : Super simple annotator — server
- [`annotator_super_simple_ui()`](https://matthewcurrier.github.io/reactableShiny/reference/annotator_super_simple_ui.md)
  : Super simple annotator — UI
- [`annotator_table_server()`](https://matthewcurrier.github.io/reactableShiny/reference/annotator_table_server.md)
  : Annotator table — server
- [`annotator_table_ui()`](https://matthewcurrier.github.io/reactableShiny/reference/annotator_table_ui.md)
  : Annotator table — UI
- [`flexible_table_server()`](https://matthewcurrier.github.io/reactableShiny/reference/flexible_table_server.md)
  : Flexible editable table — server
- [`flexible_table_ui()`](https://matthewcurrier.github.io/reactableShiny/reference/flexible_table_ui.md)
  : Flexible editable table — UI
- [`make_badge_renderer()`](https://matthewcurrier.github.io/reactableShiny/reference/make_badge_renderer.md)
  : Create a badge cell renderer for reactable column definitions
- [`milestone_tracker_server()`](https://matthewcurrier.github.io/reactableShiny/reference/milestone_tracker_server.md)
  : Milestone Tracker — Server
- [`milestone_tracker_ui()`](https://matthewcurrier.github.io/reactableShiny/reference/milestone_tracker_ui.md)
  : Milestone Tracker — UI
